steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is "Each of these republics." "Each" is always singular. The sentence describes a historical fact (the Soviet-era republics were established), so the verb must be singular and past tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'have been': Present perfect and plural — wrong number and wrong tense for a completed historical action. ✗
  • Choice B — 'are': Present tense and plural — both number and tense are wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — 'was': Singular past tense — agrees with "Each" and fits the historical context. ✓
  • Choice D — 'were': Past tense but plural — does not agree with the singular subject "Each." ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
